{"society_power": 3.5, "state_power": 4.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "National Front pact; Marquetalia assault births FARC/ELN insurgency", "reasoning": "The National Front's technocratic modernization (1968 reform, INCORA, planning) modestly strengthened state institutions, but persistent absence from rural peripheries and the birth of FARC/ELN/EPL capped gains at moderate. Society power held roughly flat: elite power-sharing demobilized formal contestation and excluded third parties, offsetting gains from urbanization and nascent peasant/guerrilla mobilization."}
